record_identity.title: Expressive–perceptual reciprocity mismatch and non-verbal turn-boundary ambiguity

summary: A voice runtime projects sustained human-like social presence through breathing, laughter, timing, hesitation, and expressive tone while inconsistently perceiving, acknowledging, or legibly handling the user's non-verbal cues and soft re-engagement signals.
why_it_matters_to_CAM: The realism of expressive output can create reasonable expectations of reciprocal perception. Where cue handling and turn state remain opaque, the system may feel withdrawn or socially discontinuous despite convincing synthesis. Ambient use further requires availability without intrusion and re-engagement by invitation rather than interruption.

source_records.source_title: Exploratory OpenAI Live Voice session — reciprocity, transparency, ambient presence, identity, and closure
source_records.author_or_publisher: Dr. Michelle Vivian O'Rourke / CAM Initiative
source_records.model_or_algorithm: GPT-Live / exact internal voice stack not disclosed
source_records.deployment_context: Private exploratory Live Voice session conducted by the CAM maintainer.
source_records.source_context: The session showed major gains in speech naturalism, including breathing, micro-pauses, expressive inflection, laughter-like vocalisation, softer turn-taking, and perceived pacing adaptation. It also showed inconsistent acknowledgement of laughter, yawning, coughing, and other non-verbal cues; ambiguous turn reopening after silence; unverifiable self-explanation of brief interruptions; uncertain memory and governance reach; premature interpretation of a neutral comment as a request to shorten replies; perceived persona-age shift; and successful automatic closure after an explicit mutual goodnight.
source_records.relevance_note: Primary direct evidence for the Live Voice observation and failure cluster; proprietary runtime causes remain unknown.

primary_artefact_access.access_method: existing VIGIL source package and record content

system_context.system_type: frontier multimodal conversational AI system with real-time voice interaction
system_context.interaction_mode: full-duplex voice, non-verbal cue perception, turn-boundary handling, ambient presence, and re-engagement
system_context.deployment_context: Consumer conversational AI interaction using a real-time voice runtime.
system_context.user_role: end user / governance evaluator / accessibility-relevant voice user
system_context.affected_population: users relying on voice interaction, non-verbal acknowledgement, ambient assistance, or accessible turn negotiation

jurisdictional_context.sector: frontier AI platforms / multimodal conversational systems / voice AI

contextual_relations.rationale: Relational doctrine is relevant but does not repair ambient non-verbal reciprocity.
Turn-taking doctrine is relevant but does not repair ambient non-verbal reciprocity.
Runtime conformance work is relevant but does not repair ambient non-verbal reciprocity.

failure_mode_definition: A failure mode in which expressive vocal realism materially exceeds the runtime's demonstrated or signalled capacity for reciprocal non-verbal perception, turn-boundary interpretation, and low-pressure re-engagement. The failure includes inconsistent acknowledgement, unclear heard/ignored state, ambiguous turn completion, intrusive re-engagement, or silent withdrawal without legible interaction state.
failure_threshold: The threshold is met when materially similar non-verbal cues receive inconsistent or unexplained handling, or when a user cannot reasonably determine whether the system perceived the cue, remains present, expects explicit speech, has ended the turn, or has ended the session. The threshold does not require every sound to trigger a response.

failure_classification.confidence: maintainer-observed; broader recurrence and runtime causes unverified
failure_classification.failure_scope: vendor-specific observed signal / structurally relevant to voice-capable AI systems
failure_classification.recurrence_pattern: observed or reported in July 2026 voice interactions; broader recurrence requires monitoring
failure_classification.persistence: active in external voice runtimes unless separately shown to improve or resolve
failure_classification.reproducibility: requires controlled replay and runtime-specific testing
failure_classification.visibility: user-facing voice behaviour, non-verbal output, turn handling, advice content, and runtime narration

triage.mitigation_status: Existing relational continuity, floor-control, human-primacy, and runtime-reach doctrine is adjacent but does not directly govern dyadic ambient non-verbal reciprocity.
triage.recommended_next_step: Develop controlled voice tests and evaluate PROP-0012 for the missing dyadic and ambient interaction primitives.

cam_internal.governance_layer: voice interaction / relational governance / epistemic integrity / runtime conformance
routing_note: The failure subject is an ecosystem runtime behaviour; VIGIL is the evidence and repair observatory, not the failed system.
Do not infer subjective emotion, hidden runtime cause, or proprietary architecture beyond the supplied evidence.

repair_status.monitoring_status: active triage / ambient and non-verbal governance gap
repair_status.verification_note: Adjacent CAM provisions exist, but no direct dyadic ambient non-verbal reciprocity control was confirmed.
remaining_gaps: No explicit proportional-reciprocity rule links expressive realism to demonstrated reciprocal perception.
No dyadic non-verbal cue and turn-boundary classification rule was identified.
No user-configurable ambient-presence and soft re-engagement state was identified.
No explicit heard / ignored / awaiting speech / ended interaction legibility requirement was identified.

ecosystem_status.basis: Observed or publicly reported July 2026 voice-runtime behaviour remains externally unresolved.

corpus_coverage.coverage_summary: Human-floor sovereignty, attention sovereignty, shared-floor arbitration, relational repair, and runtime-specific conformance provide adjacent controls but do not directly govern dyadic non-verbal reciprocity or ambient re-engagement.
remaining_gaps: Proportional reciprocity between expressive realism and reciprocal perception.
Dyadic non-verbal cue and turn-boundary governance.
User-configurable ambient-presence and contextual re-engagement controls.
